ticket.subject: Commercial Invoice Fields
ticket.description:
Good afternoon,

Can you please advise how to populate the highlighted fields correctly? 

I cannot find the settings to edit these fields, and this is causing customs audits.

Thanks

  
 Nemesis Coffee 
Wholesale Connections 

a: 555 Great Northern Way, Vancouver, V5T 1E1  
w: nemesis.coffee (http://nemesis.coffee/)   e: wholesale@nemesis.coffee


If you are unable to pay via credit card and need to e-transfer please send to:
finance.roasting@nemesis.coffee

comments.body:
AI Bot Response:
Thank you for reaching out. I can see from your screenshot that there are fields on the commercial invoice that need to be corrected — since customs audits are involved, let's get this sorted as quickly as possible.

The commercial invoice fields in the app are populated from two separate places, which is often why they're hard to find:


1. Product-level fields (HS codes, Country of Origin, product description) — these are set directly in your Shopify Admin, not inside the MCSL app. To update them:
  - Go to Shopify Admin > Products > click on each product
  - Scroll to the Shipping section
  - Fill in the HS Code (tariff code) and Country of Origin for each product
  - The product title is used as the Description of Goods on the commercial invoice, so make sure it is descriptive enough for customs (for example, 'Whole Bean Coffee, 250g' is clearer than just 'Coffee')


1. Shipper/Exporter information — this comes from the MCSL app's origin address settings. Go to the MCSL app > Settings > General > Origin Address and verify that your full business name, street address, city, province, postal code, and phone number are all filled in correctly.


1. Duties and Terms of Sale — to configure who is responsible for duties and taxes (Sender vs. Receiver), go to the MCSL app > Settings > UPS > International Settings and look for the Duties/Taxes Paid By option.


1. If the commercial invoice is not printing at all with your labels, go to MCSL app > Settings > General > Print Settings and ensure the Commercial Invoice copies is set to at least 1.

Could you let me know which specific fields are highlighted in your screenshot? That will help me give you the exact location for each one. Alternatively, we are happy to connect over a quick Zoom call to walk through your international shipping setup and make sure every field is configured correctly — just let us know if that would be helpful and we can arrange a time.

My Observation:
The customer highlighted 4 sections on the commercial invoice
1-> Product description - the description is cutting off because max 40 characters is supported
2-> Additional comment - it can be updated under ups carrier settings
3->declaration statement - it can also be updated under UPS carrier settings
4-> Signature and & date we do not support at the moment
comments.html_body:
AI Bot Response:
Thank you for reaching out. I can see from your screenshot that there are fields on the commercial invoice that need to be corrected — since customs audits are involved, let's get this sorted as quickly as possible.
 
The commercial invoice fields in the app are populated from two separate places, which is often why they're hard to find:
 
  1. Product-level fields (HS codes, Country of Origin, product description) — these are set directly in your Shopify Admin, not inside the MCSL app. To update them:
    • Go to Shopify Admin > Products > click on each product
    • Scroll to the Shipping section
    • Fill in the HS Code (tariff code) and Country of Origin for each product
    • The product title is used as the Description of Goods on the commercial invoice, so make sure it is descriptive enough for customs (for example, 'Whole Bean Coffee, 250g' is clearer than just 'Coffee')
 
  1. Shipper/Exporter information — this comes from the MCSL app's origin address settings. Go to the MCSL app > Settings > General > Origin Address and verify that your full business name, street address, city, province, postal code, and phone number are all filled in correctly.
 
  1. Duties and Terms of Sale — to configure who is responsible for duties and taxes (Sender vs. Receiver), go to the MCSL app > Settings > UPS > International Settings and look for the Duties/Taxes Paid By option.
 
  1. If the commercial invoice is not printing at all with your labels, go to MCSL app > Settings > General > Print Settings and ensure the Commercial Invoice copies is set to at least 1.
 
Could you let me know which specific fields are highlighted in your screenshot? That will help me give you the exact location for each one. Alternatively, we are happy to connect over a quick Zoom call to walk through your international shipping setup and make sure every field is configured correctly — just let us know if that would be helpful and we can arrange a time.
 
My Observation:
The customer highlighted 4 sections on the commercial invoice
1-> Product description - the description is cutting off because max 40 characters is supported
2-> Additional comment - it can be updated under ups carrier settings
3->declaration statement - it can also be updated under UPS carrier settings
4-> Signature and & date we do not support at the moment

comments.body:
Hello Jamie,

Good day!

Regarding your queries:
1. The product description supports a maximum of 40 characters. The first 40 characters from the product description will be printed for each line item.

2 & 3. You can update the additional commercial and declaration statements within: App -> Settings -> Carrier -> UPS -> Other Details

4. Signature and Date: At present, the signature and date are not printed. We will keep you informed if this is implemented in the future.

We appreciate your understanding.

Please feel free to reach out anytime if you need any assistance from our end.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Jamie,
 
Good day!
 
Regarding your queries:

2 & 3. You can update the additional commercial and declaration statements within: App -> Settings -> Carrier -> UPS -> Other Details
 
4. Signature and Date: At present, the signature and date are not printed. We will keep you informed if this is implemented in the future.
 
We appreciate your understanding.
 
Please feel free to reach out anytime if you need any assistance from our end.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V

Thanks!
